seasonArc: Lorena Wiebes remained the benchmark sprinter of the 2026 women's spring, even on days the climbs denied her. As defending Milano-Sanremo Donne champion she was briefly distanced on the Cipressa and caught behind the decisive Poggio split, then led the chasing peloton home for sixth at +0:09 — the best of the rest behind a five-rider break that included her own teammate and eventual winner Lotte Kopecky. She found her own win at the Copenhagen Sprint Women and was a fixture in the cobbled-classics finales, taking sixth at Paris-Roubaix Femmes (+2:20), a spring that confirmed her enduring status as the fastest finisher whenever the race stayed together.
